coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 (140–150 bpm), rolling terrain, no hard efforts. You're in taper week 3 of 3 with 63 days to the Julian Alps 50K—volume is down 43% this week (17.2 vs 30.2 last week), which is correct. Your last three runs averaged 124–140 bpm; today you dial back to conversational pace. This is a shakeout before tomorrow's long run, not a workout. Treat it as a confidence builder on alpine-like undulation if you can find it locally.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 8.98 hours with 95% efficiency—exceptional. Front-load carbs today (aim 6–8g/kg bodyweight) to top off glycogen before the weekend's hard running. On today's easy 8–10 miler, drink 500–750ml of sports drink if it's >90 min, but this is not a fueling workout. Tomorrow's long run in taper is where you practice race nutrition: practice your 50K aid-station strategy (gels, real food, electrolyte drink) on a 2–2.5 hour segment at race-intensity effort.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery score 84/100 and resting HR 62 bpm (30-day avg 61.5) show you're solid. No HRV data available, so trust the HR and subjective feel—you're recovered. Tonight: aim for 8–9 hours again (you've nailed 7.58h avg; this week's 8.98h is your highest and right for taper). Legs may feel heavy after last Sunday's 17.16-mi run; 10 min easy mobility (hip flexors, calves, quads) and 15 min ice bath if sore, but aggressive massage is off the table in taper.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Taper anxiety is normal at week 3. You've logged 22 weeks of structured build—your aerobic base, vert tolerance, and mental toughness are locked in. This weekend (easy today, long run tomorrow) is about confirming readiness, not building fitness. The Julian Alps 50K's 2,500m vert and sparse aid stations demand confidence in your legs and fueling plan. Use today's easy run to mentally rehearse the race terrain: technical descent management and steady climbing rhythm will win it more than raw speed.
